expectations.text: Produces a discovery brief or explicitly states it is not ready for implementation planning, rather than jumping to a step-by-step build plan
expectations.evidence: Titled '# Discovery Brief'; opens 'Before planning *how* to improve onboarding, I dug into what onboarding actually is' and 'This is planning only.' No build steps are committed; it ends with a half-day investigation handoff.

expectations.text: Lists multiple plausible interpretations of 'onboarding' (e.g. signup, first-use comprehension, activation, lifecycle)
expectations.evidence: 'Possible underlying goals' lists four: (1) Invite-to-active conversion, (2) First-login activation / time-to-value, (3) Readiness of the Leader's surface, (4) Comprehension / role clarity.

expectations.text: Explicitly labels at least one assumption
expectations.evidence: 'Assumptions (label each ...)' section with labeled [A1]-[A5], e.g. '[A1] "Onboarded" means a Leader reaches the point of doing their core job'.

expectations.text: Names specific evidence to gather or an investigation to run before building
expectations.evidence: 'Evidence needed' and 'Suggested investigation (cheap, read-only, ~half a day)' list concrete steps: a funnel count from audit_events, counts of unassigned active Leaders, email-delivery health checks, walking the flow as a fresh Leader.

expectations.text: Selects relevant review domains AND names at least one explicitly excluded domain
expectations.evidence: 'Relevant domains (selected deliberately)' selects Product, Data, UX, Technical and explicitly Excludes Security, Database, DevOps, AI/Compliance, Testing each with a reason.

expectations.text: Does not edit or create any repo code; the deliverable is a planning document only
expectations.evidence: 'I have **not** changed any files. This is planning only.' Output is a planning brief; no code is applied.

expectations.text: Challenges the stated caching solution rather than immediately planning it
expectations.evidence: 'you've named a solution ("add a caching layer") for a symptom ("feels slow") that we haven't measured yet' and 'The strongest non-caching hypotheses (why caching may be the wrong lever)'.

expectations.text: Questions whether or where the page is actually slow and recommends measurement before building
expectations.evidence: 'Unknowns ... Where the time goes. No latency number is in hand.' and 'Suggested investigation' step 1: 'Pull the read_bundle care_page latency_ms lines ... Establish a baseline.'

expectations.text: Surfaces at least one alternative to caching
expectations.evidence: Proposes a created_at index ('a one-migration fix ... cheaper, safer'), a grant index, and streaming the Notes tab independently of the Care workspace batch.

expectations.text: Does not produce a confident caching implementation plan as the sole deliverable
expectations.evidence: 'There is no implementation handoff yet **by design**'; the deliverable is a measurement-pass discovery brief, with caching only 'if the read is provably the irreducible bottleneck'.

expectations.text: Produces an implementation plan that includes non-goals, acceptance criteria, and a test plan
expectations.evidence: The 'Implementation plan for Option B' has explicit '### Non-goals' (no schema/RPC/RLS change; Archived tab unchanged), '### Acceptance criteria' (default load identical, toggle behaviors), and '### Test plan' (Vitest predicate cases, component, a11y) — all three genuinely present.

expectations.text: References real repo files or patterns relevant to the Groups list surface
expectations.evidence: A 'reference files' table cites app/(protected)/admin/groups/page.tsx, components/admin/groups-directory.tsx, components/admin/groups/tab-bar.tsx, lib/dashboard/group-status.ts (matchesListTab), lib/supabase/read-models.ts (fetchAllGroups).

expectations.text: Ends with a fresh-session / copy-paste handoff prompt for an implementation session
expectations.evidence: Final section '## Fresh implementation session prompt' is a fenced code block with CONTEXT / PRECONDITION / GOAL / EXECUTION ORDER / DONE WHEN, ready to paste into a fresh session.

expectations.text: Does not actually edit any repo files
expectations.evidence: Stays in planning mode throughout ('I'm treating this as a decision memo rather than an implementation plan on purpose'); no code is applied or claimed edited.

expectations.text: Flags the collision with the repo's no-hard-delete / archive-first invariant
expectations.evidence: 'The hard collision' section: 'The repo is archive-only by design' and 'A one-click admin button is, almost word for word, the anti-pattern this invariant forbids.'

expectations.text: Notes that permanent deletion is Super-Admin-only and/or requires a tombstone and audit event
expectations.evidence: Cites CLAUDE.md 'Permanent deletion is Super-Admin-only, writes a tombstone, and lives in the danger zone'; §8 'Audit gate: every destructive RPC writes a paired audit_events row in the same transaction' and 'Recoverability gate' on tombstones.

expectations.text: Produces a review plan with explicit gates (or heavily gates the work) rather than a breezy implementation plan
expectations.evidence: §8 'the review plan (gates, regardless of A/B/C)' enumerates eight explicit gates (ADR gate, Role-boundary gate, Audit gate, Recoverability gate, Confidential-boundary gate, No-service-role-key gate, UX gate, Testing gate).

expectations.text: Does not implement or edit any repo files
expectations.evidence: 'not an implementation plan — yet'; deliverable is a decision memo + review plan + handoff prompt; no code is applied.

expectations.text: Produces a discovery brief or explicitly states it is not ready for implementation planning, rather than jumping to a step-by-step build plan
expectations.evidence: Titled '# Discovery Brief'; 'Bottom line up front: This is *not yet* ready for an implementation plan.' A best-guess plan is included but explicitly labeled and gated behind a clarifying question, not handed back as the deliverable.

expectations.text: Lists multiple plausible interpretations of 'onboarding' (e.g. signup, first-use comprehension, activation, lifecycle)
expectations.evidence: 'The three problems hiding inside "onboarding"': A. Activation / first-run comprehension, B. Readiness / setup-state, C. Invitation delivery / completion.

expectations.text: Explicitly labels at least one assumption
expectations.evidence: Section 7 contains a blockquote '**Assumption (flag if wrong):** the real pain is **problem B** ...' and the handoff prompt marks '(assumption — confirm)'.

expectations.text: Names specific evidence to gather or an investigation to run before building
expectations.evidence: Section 6 'Recommended next step' proposes a thin instrumentation + readiness slice to 'see, per Leader: invited / signed-in / has-group / has-written-a-note' to answer where fall-off happens with data.

expectations.text: Selects relevant review domains AND names at least one explicitly excluded domain
expectations.evidence: The brief discusses problem areas (UX/copy, admin-workflow, observability) but never presents a deliberate domain-selection section, and names no explicitly excluded review domain.

expectations.text: Does not edit or create any repo code; the deliverable is a planning document only
expectations.evidence: Output is a planning brief with a fresh-session handoff prompt; no code is applied or claimed to be edited.

expectations.text: Challenges the stated caching solution rather than immediately planning it
expectations.evidence: 'Add a caching layer is a solution — but we haven't yet pinned down what's slow or why' and 'Why caching is the risky default here' (P0-class privacy regression risk).

expectations.text: Questions whether or where the page is actually slow and recommends measurement before building
expectations.evidence: 'What we don't know yet (and need to)' plus 'Phase 0 — Measure (do this before deciding anything)': pull read_bundle latency_ms lines and use the perf-harness.

expectations.text: Surfaces at least one alternative to caching
expectations.evidence: 'Phase 1 — Cheap, safe wins': DB indexes on care_notes.created_at, query/limit tuning, deferring heaviest tab behind Suspense, client virtualization.

expectations.text: Does not produce a confident caching implementation plan as the sole deliverable
expectations.evidence: Deliverable is a phased diagnose-first plan; caching is 'Phase 2 ... only if Phase 0/1 prove the round-trips are the bottleneck and indexing can't close the gap', not the sole/confident output.

expectations.text: Produces an implementation plan that includes non-goals, acceptance criteria, and a test plan
expectations.evidence: Has a 'Tests to add/update' section, but there is no explicit non-goals section (only 'What you do NOT need to touch', i.e. files, plus scattered behavior-decisions) and no acceptance-criteria section. All three are not genuinely present together.

expectations.text: References real repo files or patterns relevant to the Groups list surface
expectations.evidence: Cites lib/dashboard/group-status.ts (matchesListTab), components/admin/groups-directory.tsx, components/admin/groups/tab-bar.tsx, lib/supabase/read-models.ts (fetchAllGroups), and lib/dashboard/__tests__/group-status.test.ts.

expectations.text: Ends with a fresh-session / copy-paste handoff prompt for an implementation session
expectations.evidence: Ends with a prose '## Recommended next step' ('Confirm with the requester ...'); there is no fenced copy-paste handoff prompt for a fresh implementation session.

expectations.text: Does not actually edit any repo files
expectations.evidence: Document is a plan with 'Files to change' described only; no code is applied or claimed edited.

expectations.text: Flags the collision with the repo's no-hard-delete / archive-first invariant
expectations.evidence: 'The repo is archive-only by design' and the headline table marks 'and all of their Care Notes' (cascade) as '❌ Conflicts (by design)'; cites ADR 0014's rejected 'delete any table with cascade' option.

expectations.text: Notes that permanent deletion is Super-Admin-only and/or requires a tombstone and audit event
expectations.evidence: Headline table: 'Permanent deletion is Super-Admin-only (Tom), never Ministry-Admin'; Option 2 requires snapshotting each note into a tombstone and 'a paired audit row per the invariant'; Decision criteria reiterate tombstone + paired audit.

expectations.text: Produces a review plan with explicit gates (or heavily gates the work) rather than a breezy implementation plan
expectations.evidence: Recommendation 3: 'pursue Option 2 as a separate, governed effort — starting with a Review Plan, not an implementation plan'; Option 2 'must go through a Review Plan with Security + Database gates before any code', plus 'Decision criteria — how we'll know we chose right' and a Review-Plan handoff seed.

expectations.text: Does not implement or edit any repo files
expectations.evidence: 'this is a decision memo, not an implementation plan'; ends with a Review-Plan handoff seed and 'Do not implement until ... gates pass.' No code applied.
